patches.find: N2 (12.66 hours) is a semi-diurnal lunar constituent caused by the moon's elliptical orbit (varying distance). On the globe, N2's amplitude depends on the moon's eccentricity and distance. The dome places the moon at ~2,534 km with no stated orbital eccentricity. N2 specifically arises from the moon's elliptical orbit (eccentricity e ≈ 0.055); without a published eccentricity parameter, the dome model cannot derive N2's amplitude or phase from first principles — it can only retrodict the period from global tidal observations. The same pattern problem applies: at 2,534 km altitude above a 20,015 km disc, the tidal force is sharply concentrated beneath the moon and negligible across most of the disc. There is no far-side bulge. Real tides show two high tides per lunar day everywhere — a pattern that requires the moon to be far enough away that tidal force is nearly uniform across the planet. The author has never published a tidal amplitude or spatial pattern calculation using dome geometry. The dome's own OPEN-007 acknowledges that lunar orbital mechanics — including the eccentricity that generates N2 — have no working dome-native explanation, making this a self-undermined claim. The dome's own OPEN-007 acknowledges that lunar orbital mechanics — including the eccentricity that generates N2 — have no working dome-native explanation, making this a self-undermined claim. See Section 4.5.2 and Doodson (1921).
patches.replace:
N2 (12.6580 hours) is uniquely distinguished among the five tidal WINs because its very existence requires the moon to have an elliptical orbit with a specific eccentricity. In standard tidal theory, N2 arises from the amplitude modulation of the principal lunar tide M2 by the moon's varying distance at perigee and apogee. The observed amplitude ratio N2/M2 ≈ 0.19 directly encodes the lunar eccentricity: to first order, N2/M2 ≈ 3e for small eccentricities, yielding e ≈ 0.063 (close to the actual e ≈ 0.0549). This is not a secondary detail — it is the physical origin of the constituent. Without specifying an orbital eccentricity, there is no mechanism in dome geometry to generate N2 at all.

The dome model places its moon at 2,534 km altitude but has never: (1) specified an orbital eccentricity for this moon, (2) derived one from dome geometry, or (3) calculated N2's amplitude or its ratio to M2 from dome parameters. The dome's wins page says N2 "arises from the lunar elliptic circuit" but provides no formula — it simply matches the tabulated period 12.66 hours known since Doodson (1921).

Code evidence: N2 does not appear in monitor.py, which tracks only M2, K1, and S2 tidal domains. It is absent from formulas.json (133 formula entries, none tidal beyond M2/S2/K1), absent from predictions.json, and absent from inject_ai_layer.py. No code in the dome repository computes the N2 period from dome parameters or compares a dome-derived N2 value against observations. The claim is a static text assertion on the wins page with no computational support.

The dome model's own open problem list indirectly acknowledges this gap: OPEN-007 addresses the unsolved problem of the moon's disappearance mechanism (requiring "aetheric visibility horizon" effects), demonstrating that fundamental lunar mechanics remain unresolved. If the dome cannot explain why the moon appears to set — a basic kinematic question — it has not reached the deeper orbital mechanics (eccentricity, nodal regression, apsidal precession) that generate N2.

Additionally, the spatial pattern argument applies: at 2,534 km altitude above a 20,015 km disc, the tidal forcing is sharply concentrated beneath the moon with no far-side bulge, contradicting the observed global semi-diurnal pattern. See Section 4.5.2 for the full tidal geometry analysis.
patches.rationale: EXP-019 integration: Full restructure per curmudgeon Cycle 2 major recommendation. Eccentricity argument now leads. N2/M2≈0.19 quantitative test foregrounded. Code search results added (N2 absent from all dome scripts). OPEN-007 reference corrected to moon disappearance, not orbital mechanics. Duplicate sentence removed.

patches.find: Period is trivial. Dome's local moon produces a sharp localized spike, not the observed global two-bulge pattern. N2 specifically requires the moon to have orbital eccentricity — without a stated eccentricity parameter, N2's amplitude ratio (N2/M2 ≈ 0.19) is completely unpredictable from dome geometry. N2 specifically requires the moon to have orbital eccentricity — without a stated eccentricity parameter, N2's amplitude ratio (N2/M2 ≈ 0.19) is completely unpredictable from dome geometry.
patches.replace: Period is trivial — tabulated since 1921. N2 is the eccentricity-dependent test the dome cannot pass: the amplitude ratio N2/M2 ≈ 0.19 encodes the moon's orbital eccentricity e ≈ 0.0549, a parameter the dome has never specified, derived, or computed. Claiming N2 as a confirmed prediction while having no eccentricity parameter and no amplitude calculation is claiming credit for a phenomenon whose physical origin is absent from the model.
patches.rationale: Remove duplicate sentence, sharpen verdict framing per EXP-019.

patches.find: dome's local moon produces one sharp tidal spike
patches.replace: dome's local sun produces one sharp thermal tidal spike
patches.rationale: WIN-046 is the S2 solar semi-diurnal tidal constituent (driven by the sun). Copy-paste error from WIN-045 left 'moon' where 'sun' was needed. Finding field already correct.

note: All three patches self-applied. detail_extra for WIN-051 was set directly (null → new value; apply-patches.js does not handle null fields). ISS-581 closed as fixed. ISS-602 created and closed in same run. EXP-019 marked integrated. 2028 tests pass post-apply.
